title: rubus vulgaris subsp. clethraphilus
intro: Rubus vulgaris subsp. clethraphilus, also known as the clethraphilus bramble, is a subspecies of the common raspberry plant, characterized by its distinctive leaf shape and habit. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of Rubus vulgaris subsp. clethraphilus have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. The herbal preparations of Rubus vulgaris subsp. clethraphilus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically as a poultice or salve to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
